ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported
e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2, Daily

ElementDescription: When you were growing up, prior to your 18th birthday: Did you have someone who loved you unconditionally (you did not doubt that they cared about you)?
When you were growing up, prior to your 18th birthday: Did you have at least one best friend (someone you could trust, had fun with)?

When you were growing up, prior to your 18th birthday: Did you do anything regularly to help others (e.g., volunteer at a hospital, nursing home, church) or do special projects in the community to help others (food drives, Habitat for Humanity)?

When you were growing up, prior to your 18th birthday: Were you regularly involved in organized sports groups (e.g., soccer, basketball, track) or other physical activity (e.g., competitive cheer, gymnastics, dance, marching band)?
When you were growing up, prior to your 18th birthday: Were you an active member of at least one civic group or a non- sport social group such as scouts, church, or youth group?

When you were growing up, prior to your 18th birthday: Did you have an engaging hobby -- an artistic or intellectual pastime either alone or in a group (e.g., chess club, debate team, musical instrument or vocal group, theater, spelling bee, or did you read a lot)?

When you were growing up, prior to your 18th birthday: Was there an adult (not your parent) you trusted and could count on when you needed help or advice (e.g., coach, teacher, minister, neighbor, relative)?
When you were growing up, prior to your 18th birthday: Was your home typically clean AND safe with enough food to eat?
When you were growing up, prior to your 18th birthday: Overall, did your schools provide the resources and academic experiences you needed to learn?
When you were growing up, prior to your 18th birthday: In your home, were there rules that were clear and fairly administered?
